description: A full program that imports a single-argument C math function, wraps it in a shape + action that calls it in a loop, and a program that runs it. Validated against the real transpiler across 20 variations (different C functions: floor, cos, sin, ceil, fabs, exp, log, ...), all parse/emit/compile clean.

alias.desc: the Dictum-side name bound to the import, conventionally `c_{c_func}` (e.g. "c_sqrt") but supplied explicitly rather than derived, since the validated data always gives it separately

label.desc: a human-friendly name for the function used only in the final result-print line -- may differ from {c_func} (e.g. c_func="cos" but label="cosine", c_func="fabs" but label="absolute value")

shape_name.desc: name for the (currently unused-by-logic but declared) result shape

arg_value.desc: the input value passed to the C function

loop_bound.desc: how many iterations the demonstration loop runs

template:
import from C the action {c_func} takes decimal number produces decimal number as {alias}

shape {shape_name} holds
    Input as decimal number
    Output as decimal number
end shape

action {action_name} produces nothing
    keep X as decimal number with value {arg_value}
    keep Result as decimal number with value 0.0
    keep I as whole number with value 0
    call {alias} with X giving Result
    while I is less than {loop_bound} repeat
        print the text "iteration" and I and newline
        set I to I plus 1
    end while
    print the text "{label} result:" and Result and newline
end action

program {program_name}
    call {action_name}
end program
requires: The `import from C` declaration must list the exact real C function name and its Dictum-visible alias before either is used anywhere else in the file.
{alias}, not {c_func}, is what gets called from Dictum code (`call {alias} with X giving Result`).
common_mistakes: Do not call {c_func} directly -- only {alias} is a valid Dictum identifier for this import.
Keep the {label} used in the result-print line human-readable -- it does not need to match {c_func}/{alias} exactly (e.g. "cosine" for cos, "absolute value" for fabs).
example:
import from C the action sqrt takes decimal number produces decimal number as c_sqrt

shape MathResult holds
    Input as decimal number
    Output as decimal number
end shape

action run_math produces nothing
    keep X as decimal number with value 144.0
    keep Result as decimal number with value 0.0
    keep I as whole number with value 0
    call c_sqrt with X giving Result
    while I is less than 3 repeat
        print the text "iteration" and I and newline
        set I to I plus 1
    end while
    print the text "sqrt result:" and Result and newline
end action

program MathDemo
    call run_math
end program
